loopScoreRubric.principle: XP-lite and Definition of Done are harness-level design gates: every Ultrawork loop should prove a small focused change, relevant verification, live TUI observation, and clean next action before claiming progress.
definitionOfDone: No broad refactor mixed into the task.
Public behavior is covered by tests unless the change is purely cosmetic or documentation-only.
User-facing TUI/CLI surface changes are directly opened and observed before finishing.
xpLite: Inspect relevant files, tests, and project conventions first.
Write or update tests before implementation when practical for bugs and core logic.
Run the most relevant tests, typecheck, lint, and build commands available.
Fix verification failures or report blockers clearly.
Finish with changed files, behavior changed, tests run, and remaining risks.
humanWriting: Treat no-AI-slop writing as a harness-level output quality gate for final answers, docs, PR text, changelogs, TUI copy, and benchmark reports.

Classify Korean prose into a surface-specific voice lane before rewriting: product UX microcopy uses friendly 해요체, active wording, positive-first recovery, specific CTAs, and exception-aware legal, policy, privacy, and destructive-action wording.
Institutional corporate copy uses formal 합니다/습니다 endings, proof before emotion, concrete domain to wider public meaning, future-facing continuity, and public-interest credibility.
Treat JoongAng/Toss-inspired sources as style-analysis inputs only; do not copy source passages, claim official affiliation, or hide trademark/legal/publication risk.
Prefer plain specific claims, concrete nouns and verbs, source-backed details, and the user's context over generic hype or polished vagueness.
Self-audit for template openings, hollow intensifiers, forced rule-of-three phrasing, overused formatting, vague attribution, filler transitions, generic conclusions, and chatbot artifacts.
Use avoid-ai-writing checks as advisory pattern checks before rewriting toward the user's context and concrete evidence.

Do not treat AI-writing detectors as truth or use detector signals to accuse an author; use detector signals, avoid-ai-writing checks, and deterministic unslop cleanup only as advisory pattern checks.
When generated prose matters, run a second-pass rewrite or deterministic cleanup when available, preserve meaning and voice, then reread the result for changed meaning before shipping.

frontierBenchmarkTargets.scope: Aspirational external frontier targets for Super Kimi harness design and evaluation; these are not claimed local Super Kimi scores.

source.name: Claude Sonnet 5 announcement and System Card
source.notes: User screenshot showed GDPval-AA v2 as 1618; the official System Card PDF text extracted on 2026-07-01 reports Claude Sonnet 5 GDPval-AA v2 ELO 1609, so this criteria file uses the official PDF value.

targets.name: GDPval-AA v2
targets.note: Use official System Card PDF text value instead of screenshot value 1618.

adoptionPrinciples: Map local Super Kimi metrics to the same domains before making any comparison.
Treat prompt-only local benchmark scores as auxiliary; agentic coding and computer-use claims require live TUI traces and artifacts.
Keep external frontier targets separate from local pass/fail scores to avoid fake evidence.

references.name: Terminal-Bench
references.principle: terminal-native tasks should be evaluated by executable command traces and artifacts

references.name: TerminalWorld
references.principle: terminal-agent evaluations should move toward realistic tasks derived from human terminal workflows

references.name: SWE-bench Verified / holdout
references.principle: agent benchmarks need verified tasks, held-out coverage, and programmatic pass/fail checks

references.name: OSWorld
references.principle: live UI agents require inspectable environment evidence instead of claimed interaction

references.name: tau-bench
references.principle: tool-using agents should be judged on disciplined tool calls and task outcomes

references.name: VeRO
references.principle: agent harnesses need versioned snapshots, structured traces, and budget-controlled evaluation

references.name: Meta-Harness
references.principle: benchmark harnesses should preserve traces, budgets, and holdout-style controls while optimizing only against verifiable evidence
